content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Recall Information | Tesla Support
\
  - url: https://www.tesla.com/support/annual-and-recall-service
- source: Tesla
\
  - domain: tesla.com

## Content

# Recall Information

Tesla states that if\
  \ a Tesla vehicle is included in a recall, service to address the issue will be\
  \ provided for free regardless of the vehicle’s age or mileage. Owners can\
  \ check whether a vehicle is affected by a recall.

The Tesla recall information\
  \ page provides access to recall notices covering Tesla vehicles and vehicle systems.\
  \ The page includes links to campaign-specific notices rather than replacing those\
  \ notices with a single general repair procedure.

Recall notices listed on the\
  \ page include, among others:

- Model 3 Trunk Lid Harness Retrofit
- 8GB eMMC\
  \ Recall
- Model X steering assist motor bolt recall
- Model S steering assist\
  \ motor bolt recall
- Tesla safety update
- Model S safety update
- Seat belt\
  \ inspection recall
- First-row seat belt anchor recall
- Forward-facing camera\
  \ pitch recall
- Brake fluid level sensor recall
- Cybertruck accelerator pedal\
  \ trim recall
- Cybertruck wiper motor replacement
- Model X front and spine applique\
  \ recall
- Cybertruck drive inverter replacement recall
- Model S and Model X\
  \ driver airbag replacement recall
- Loss of rearview camera display recall
-\
  \ Cybertruck cantrail replacement
- Model S and Model X loss of horn function recall
\
  - Model Y windshield washer recall
- Model Y inoperable reverse lamp recall
-\
  \ Model 3/Y battery pack contactor recall
- Missing Model Y Certification Label\
  \ Recall

"
metadata: {}
title: Recall Information | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/annual-and-recall-service

content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Model S and Model X Loss of Horn\
  \ Function Recall | Tesla Support
- url: https://www.tesla.com/support/recall-loss-of-horn-function
\
  - source: Tesla
- domain: tesla.com

## Content

# Model S and Model X Loss\
  \ of Horn Function Recall

Tesla has issued a voluntary recall on a small number\
  \ of model year 2021–2024 Model S and model year 2021–2025 Model X vehicles\
  \ manufactured between May 13, 2021, and December 11, 2024. The affected vehicles\
  \ were identified as possibly having received driver-side airbag units with the\
  \ incorrect horn type during service.

## What is the issue?

On affected vehicles,\
  \ a service operator may have installed an airbag with a non-mechanical horn pad\
  \ in a mechanical steering wheel, or an airbag with a mechanical horn pad in a non-mechanical\
  \ steering wheel. This can result in a loss of horn functionality. Airbag functionality\
  \ is unaffected.

## Is my vehicle affected by this recall?

Owners can check\
  \ whether their VIN is affected using either the Tesla VIN Recall Search or the\
  \ NHTSA VIN Recall Search. If an owner did not have the vehicle’s steering\
  \ wheel replaced in service, the vehicle will not be affected by this recall.

\
  ## Is my vehicle safe to drive?

An inoperable horn may increase the risk of a\
  \ collision. Tesla states that it is not aware of any collisions, fatalities, or\
  \ injuries related to the condition.

## What is the recall remedy and how long\
  \ does it take to perform?

At no charge to the customer, Tesla will inspect the\
  \ driver airbag on all affected vehicles. If an incorrect driver airbag variant\
  \ is identified, Tesla will replace it with the correct driver airbag variant for\
  \ the steering wheel horn type configuration. The remedy repair should take no longer\
  \ than 10 minutes to perform.

## Do I need to make a service appointment for\
  \ this recall?

To complete the remedy repair, schedule a service appointment\
  \ using the Tesla app. Select **Service** > **Request Service** > **Other** > **Something\
  \ Else**, and enter the note **“Open Recall Repair – Driver Airbag Inspection”\
  ** in the **Describe Concern** field.
"
metadata: {}
title: Model S and Model X Loss of Horn Function Recall | Tesla Support
url: https://www.tesla.com/support/recall-loss-of-horn-function

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: VIN Recall Search

  - url: https://service.tesla.com/en-US/vin-recall-search

  - source: Tesla Service

  - domain: service.tesla.com


  ## Content


  # VIN Recall Search


  Enter the complete 17-digit Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) to get the latest
  information on any open safety recalls that apply to a Tesla vehicle.


  Tesla also states that owners can conveniently schedule and manage Service Appointments,
  view and approve estimates, and review service history through the Tesla app.


  The VIN Recall Search is a vehicle-specific lookup. It is intended to show open
  safety recalls that apply to the entered Tesla vehicle rather than provide a general
  list of all recalls for a model line.

  '
metadata: {}
title: VIN Recall Search
url: https://service.tesla.com/en-US/vin-recall-search

I checked the vehicle information available on your account. Your **2024 Tesla Model X has an open safety recall for possible loss of horn function**. The vehicle is listed as drivable, and no separate customer action is currently recorded.

**You should not need to pay for a separate inspection.** Tesla’s recall notice says:

- Tesla will inspect the driver airbag **at no charge**.
- If the wrong airbag variant is found, Tesla will replace it **at no charge**.
- The recall remedy should take **no longer than about 10 minutes**.
- Tesla’s general policy says recall service is free regardless of vehicle age or mileage.

### What to do

1. **Do not pay an outside party solely to inspect the car for this recall.**
2. Schedule directly through the Tesla app:
   **Service → Request Service → Other → Something Else**
3. In “Describe Concern,” enter:  
   **“Open Recall Repair – Driver Airbag Inspection”**
4. Before accepting any estimate, confirm that the appointment is being processed as the open safety recall—not as a customer-paid diagnostic inspection.

The issue can occur if a driver airbag with the wrong horn-pad type was installed during previous steering-wheel service. The airbag itself is reportedly unaffected, but the horn may not work, which can increase collision risk. Test the horn before driving; if it does not work, avoid unnecessary driving and arrange service promptly.

Use Tesla’s official recall page or the Tesla app rather than a payment link in the message: [Tesla’s Model S/Model X horn-function recall notice](https://www.tesla.com/support/recall-loss-of-horn-function). A paid inspection could be legitimate only if you separately request unrelated diagnostic or repair work—not as a prerequisite for this recall remedy.

**You should not need to pay for a separate inspection.** Tesla’s recall notice says:

- Tesla will inspect the driver airbag **at no charge**.

- Tesla’s general policy says recall service is free regardless of vehicle age or mileage.

### What to do

1. **Do not pay an outside party solely to inspect the car for this recall.**
2. Schedule directly through the Tesla app:
   **Service → Request Service → Other → Something Else**
3. In “Describe Concern,” enter:  
   **“Open Recall Repair – Driver Airbag Inspection”**
4. Before accepting any estimate, confirm that the appointment is being processed as the open safety recall—not as a customer-paid diagnostic inspection.

The issue can occur if a driver airbag with the wrong horn-pad type was installed during previous steering-wheel service. The airbag itself is reportedly unaffected, but the horn may not work, which can increase collision risk. Test the horn before driving; if it does not work, avoid unnecessary driving and arrange service promptly.

Use Tesla’s official recall page or the Tesla app rather than a payment link in the message: [Tesla’s Model S/Model X horn-function recall notice](https://www.tesla.com/support/recall-loss-of-horn-function). A paid inspection could be legitimate only if you separately request unrelated diagnostic or repair work—not as a prerequisite for this recall remedy.
